rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Kearney and Mr. Cantley taken in this case preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, Hon. Ernest H. Goldsmith will preside. = (220/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Redwood Plumbing Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 4 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation inserts attributable to defendant. Mr. Hixson¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did sustain its burden, Mr. Hixson¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation inserts attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Hixson¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs have dismissed strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, Hon. Ernest H. Goldsmith will preside. = (220/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FABIAN CARCALLAS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant General Cable Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. By failing to include responses to contention interrogatories with its moving papers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carcallas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, Hon. Ernest H. Goldsmith will preside. = (220/EHG)
